citation.description: Python docstring: 'force bypasses GATES generally — including any gate added in the future'; CLI transition.py line 43-45: '--force: Bypass any enabled gate when starting work (open->in_progress) — e.g. the plan-review gate today, and any gate added in the future'; docs/user-guide.md line 234-237: 'bypasses any enabled start-work gate... whatever gate is configured to run on claim, now or in the future'; AGENTS.md lines 63-66: '--force[=<reason>] bypasses any enabled start-work gate (plan-review or whatever gate is configured, present or future)'; MCP acknowledges force is CLI/library-only without claiming wrong behavior

criteria.criterion: Public Python, CLI, MCP, user, and agent guidance consistently states that force bypasses all gates.

citation.description: Python docstring explicitly states: 'that is a ticket-system invariant, not a gate'. CLI transition_close.py lines 507-511 say 'STRUCTURAL INTEGRITY invariant... NOT a quality gate'. CLI transition.py _USAGE line 46 says 'a structural invariant'.

criteria.criterion: Guidance explicitly classifies the open-children close prohibition as a ticket-system invariant rather than a gate.

citation.description: Python docstring: 'it does not waive the unresolved-children close prohibition: that is a ticket-system invariant, not a gate, and no force/force_close combination can close a ticket while any child remains non-closed'. CLI transition.py _USAGE line 45-46: 'Does NOT bypass the unresolved-children close guard (a structural invariant)'. transition_close.py lines 508-511: 'enforced UNCONDITIONALLY: neither --force... nor --force-close... can close a parent over open children'.

criteria.criterion: Guidance states that force does not bypass ticket-system invariants.

citation.description: CLI transition usage: 'e.g. the plan-review gate today, and any gate added in the future'. CLI claim.py line 30: 'e.g. plan-review today, and any gate added in the future'. Python docstring: 'including any gate added in the future'. User guide and AGENTS.md: 'whatever gate is configured, present or future'. No surface presents a closed, exhaustive list.

criteria.criterion: Documentation does not hardcode a closed list of current gates as the definition of force behavior.

citation.description: test_force_does_NOT_close_parent_with_open_child (line 52-64): asserts --force cannot close parent with open children and 'cannot be bypassed' appears in output. test_library_transition_force_does_NOT_close_parent_with_open_child (lines 67-79): library test, raises RebarError match='unresolved'. test_force_close_does_NOT_close_parent_with_open_child (lines 82-93). In test_plan_review_gate.py: test_force_bypasses_gate_with_audit (line 165) and test_transition_to_in_progress_force_bypasses_with_audit (line 201) demonstrate force bypasses the plan-review gate.

criteria.criterion: Contract tests demonstrate that force bypasses enabled gates but cannot close a parent while any child remains non-closed.
